theme_notes: Andy Burnham becomes the UK's seventh PM in a decade as Keir Starmer is ousted by his own party; Trump drops 50% tariffs on Canadian goods; Houthis open a naval blockade of Saudi Arabia; Spain's World Cup win settles into the cultural hangover.
settled: Even. Andy Burnham called this a 'circuit breaker.' Turns out you didn't need one.
Zero. The Houthis blockaded Saudi Arabia. Your balance is mercifully unblockaded.
All square. Canada is livid about something totally unrelated. Enjoy the moment.
youOwe: Less than Trump's tariff on a Canadian greeting card. Somehow still embarrassing.
Pocket change. The UK cycled through seven PMs before you got around to paying this.
Negligible. Like the Houthis' naval budget compared to their ambitions.
You owe about what Canada loses per hour under Trump's new 50% tariff. Pay faster.
Burnham's been PM for 24 hours and he's already more decisive than you.
Enough for a World Cup commemorative print. Of the team that actually won.
That's roughly what Canada's dairy board spends on tariff lawyers per minute.
Ferran Torres scored in the 105th minute. You haven't scored in months.
The Houthis declared a naval blockade. Your partner declared a payment deadline.
Keir Starmer lost his party, his job, and his legacy. What's your excuse?
Canada just got hit with 50% tariffs. Your partner would settle for just the money.
Enough to fly to Manchester and ask Andy Burnham for a circuit breaker. He's busy.
Russia sent 41 missiles in one night. Your partner sent 41 payment reminders. Both ongoing.
You owe enough to qualify as a hostile trade act. Canada's lawyers are calling.
That's more than Canada pays per tariff exemption application. You're in the range.
You're not their partner. You're their geopolitical adversary with shared Netflix.
Andy Burnham promised to end the cycle of failure. You are the cycle of failure.
Trump put 50% tariffs on Canada and Canada is handling it better than you are.
theyOwe: They owe you less than a UK PM lasts on average. Still — it adds up.
Smaller than Messi's gap between showing up and winning. It still matters.
Barely anything. Like the Houthis' opening move. It escalates from here.
They owe enough for a World Cup final nosebleed seat. Where were they sitting?
Burnham's been PM longer than this debt. One of those has an exit strategy.
About what Canada now owes in tariffs on a case of maple syrup. Collect with dignity.
Enough that even Messi would shrug and say: yeah, I know that feeling.
Their debt is growing faster than Keir Starmer's regrets.
Canada got hit with 50% tariffs and responded with grace. You don't have to.
They owe more than the Houthis' blockade costs in disrupted shipping lanes.
Burnham became the UK's seventh PM in a decade. They've made one decision. Poorly.
Russia launched 125 drones last night. Zero payments from them. Both: unresolved.
They owe enough that Canada would classify this as a hostile trade action.
Spain-level debt: dominant, technical, settled only in extra time.
Burnham promised a 'circuit breaker.' You could use one. For this.
They owe you more than Trump's 50% tariff costs Canada in a bad week.
At this point you are their IMF. Start setting structural adjustment conditions.
The Houthis blockaded an ocean. Your partner blockaded accountability. Escalate accordingly.
